name: Boiled Crawfish
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot, bring seasonings (salt and crab boil) to a boil for 10 minutes.
2. Add the potatoes, corn, and smoked sausage. Bring back to a boil.
3. Boil for another 10 minutes.
4. Add the crawfish and bring back to a boil.
5. Turn off the heat immediately.
6. Let soak for 20 to 30 minutes.
7. Drain the water from the pot.
8. Peel and eat the crawfish. While waiting for the water to return to a boil, cull and clean the crawfish by rinsing them well with garden hose and removing any dead ones. Always wear gloves when handling crawfish, as they can still pinch you through gloves. As Justin Wilson notes, "Not everybody likes the fat, but I do, and I love to dig my finger into the head and scoop it out." During crawfish season, his fingers stay yellow from one end to the other.
